full_text: Last question, one, six, right. So we do get them, fortunately, surgery doesn't always improve pain. And this is a very frank discussion that we have to have with everybody, who's listed for a blackoscopic procedure, whether it be their first one, or their seventh, eighth or ninth one, or whether it even be whether having his stretch to me, okay? It doesn't necessarily mean pain is what we call multimodal, right? Many things cause it, many things improve it, many things make it worse. Not one thing will answer everything, okay? So the surgery for some people may not improve it. You may not have had enough surgery, okay? You may have not have had a proper assessment. So it's worth having, you know, having that discussion after six months, why have I not improved? Should I be looking at hormones, or should do I need to be reviewed by somebody else?
